reviews.review_description: My son is in tkd so love this app to practice his forms

This app has so much potential. It’s a shame it only offers Tae Kwon Do. Would love to see an expanded catalog: a karate style, a kung style, even some weapon styles thrown in. 

Once the catalog has expanded, online vr classes could be held or even creating partnerships with instructors / dojos, where students could go get real life experience. 

I really hope this app has not been abandoned. For a busy person like me, this is what I need.

developer_response.body: Thanks for your feedback! I'm just a solo dev, but it definitely hasn't been abandoned :) I've just finished adding all Taekwondo patterns up to 6th Dan, and I'm currently looking for partners to help fund additional martial arts.

reviews.review_description: This would be awesome to learn kata for grading.  Maybe include different types of karate kata.  Im actually not sure if you have already done this. Lol. I'll take another look.

This is what VR shluld be al about! This is the Matrix we so long waited for.

Even if u have to pay to unluck the diffrent style, It is still a reasonable price 45usd for the full taekwando. 

Plz consider Krav maga, I would love a pure self defence course! But keep up the good work and plz I hope you get sponsored, cuz this is the true meaning and potential of VR! We lpve your work! GG!

Great Fitness-Training - starts slow and becomes more and more demanding. The training is impressive and works very well. Fascinating and serious workout, feels empowering. I directly bought the bundle. 

If you do not want to buy in-app one by one you can get the whole bundle a bit cheaper searching the store for: "Taekwondo Kukkiwon Poomsae Trainer". It is the same app as PoseAR just with all content bundled. -> I am not an affiliate, I just found the link very helpful myself!

every time i press the menu button it the guy stays in the same spot

fix your game devs

developer_response.body: To start each level, you must stand within the blue outline and match your hands and feet to the correct position, as indicated by the audio and visual prompts. If you're still having problems, please contact us at support@pozear.com

reviews.review_description: It’s a good game, but what has put me off is that you have to pay each time you add a new martial art, why can’t you just buy the game and get the whole game

developer_response.body: Thanks for your feedback. If it helps, I have packaged up all the Taekwondo poomsae into a 'buy one time' package here: https://meta.com/en-gb/experiences/taekwondo-kukkiwon-poomsae-trainer/9349399915167613/

reviews.review_title: Wish it included the ITF tuls
reviews.review_description: Would be a great help for perfecting the moves if it only included the ITF tuls and not just focus on WT.

While this may not grab hardened martial artists, I think there is huge potential for new trainees and the untapped market of people wanting to learn from home. This should be more successful than videos or zoom sessions.
Trainess could use this to practice at home between classes.
There are some well-developed very popular boxercise programs on Meta, and while they don't teach you to fight they do help with proper technique and a level of fitness. 

I think this has huge (and currently untapped) potential for martial arts technique and a level of fitness
And while the aficionados may disagree, this could reach a huge market that can't or aren't comfortable attending a live class.

Would love to see DLC's such as:
Wing Chun
Kick Boxing Kung Fu (and probably classical styles would interest)
To name a few :)

Best wishes for the development of this app.
Please consider a one-time payment (with possible DLC releases)
NOT a monthly subscription 

I think this is why Les Mills Body XR is so successful

developer_response.body: Thanks for your kind words! Agree, PozeAR is NOT a monthly subscription. You buy patterns as you go (i.e. as you progress through the belts in real life), and once purchased they are yours for life.

reviews.review_description: This is a great concept. There is an option to purchase/unlock different styles. I registered an account with the developer's website as directed in game to purchase the karate option. However, it did not allow me to purchase the karate version.

developer_response.body: Hi RobRotten. I'm sorry I haven't been able to record that karate version yet. I would love to add new modules but I'm just a solo developer and I'm looking for funding/a business partner to grow the project.

reviews.review_description: Great application.  Limited to only one style.  Thought it had karate which would have been amazing.
There's some fine tuning, however this is a great way to bring out the ability of what VR can be used for. I look forward to seeing this one grow. We'll done 👌

reviews.review_title: Good start, hoping to see thsi develop
reviews.review_description: Great for learning poomsae, it's not fully developed but it has potential. You might need to develop VR/AR legs for this but you can at least learn the movements. I brought it to my dojang and they thought it was an interesting app. They have poomsaes from taeguek Il jang to keumgang and taebeck and above is in development. Overall, I am very much impressed someone was able to pull off taekwondo which requires feet to work on a quest system. Make sure you have space, you dont want to punch a wall or reset your position when you run out of room.

This is pretty cool for martial arts. As an instructor, it's something I've always wanted. It takes some time getting used to, but I'm very glad that someone has taken the time and effort to build this.

reviews.review_title: Excellent app. Waiting for Tai Chi
reviews.review_description: Really good and promising. Looking forward to seeing Tai Chi added.

I can also kick and block!
For now, there is only Taekwondo, but it looks like Karate and Kung Fu will be coming in the future.

January 29, 2024　Additional edit
I have a bed in my room, and I was worried about the bed getting in the way of playing. However, I found that if I set the height to be smaller, the play area would be smaller and the bed wouldn't get in the way.
If you have the same problem as me, try reducing the height in the settings!

Some things I didn't like:
1. The orange menu that you're supposed to stand in is outside of my play space.
2. The trainer guy looks too big and cartoony.  Would be better if it was a hologram like the trailer
3. Don't like having to jump through hoops to purchase content.  Just make it a DLC that I can purchase inside the app itself.
I tried the devs suggestion but no you can't change the size of the character without changing your own height which makes everything smaller and the game unplayable.
And I definitely have enough room in my playspace.  The boundary is up against the soft which I can't stand in but I can definitely punch over it.  So the game is making assumptions.

developer_response.body:
Thank you for your kind words and helpful feedback!

1. Understood. However PozeAR teaches real Taekwondo patterns, used to grade for real belts. I didn't design these patterns and unfortunately cannot control how much room they require. The orange menu is deliberately placed so as to indicate how much play space you're going to need.
2. Okay. You can adjust the size of the trainer in the menu. The cartoony-ness I will try to improve.
3. Point taken.

reviews.review_description: Strongly recommend this VR app for any Taekowndo student who wants some extra coaching at home.   As a 1st Dan Blackbelt, the “coach” corrected some of my positioning for extra practice.   Of extra note, the training also shows you the “set” or starting position and follow through, not just the “end” position.    Amazing start to what is going to be an excellent at-home Poomsae training supplement.   If you’re training in Taekwondo and have a Quest, then this is a no-brained download to check it out.

Learning martial arts takes a lot of repetition to build up muscle memory. With this game you can have the virtual instructor next to you in your own room so that you can practice as many times as you like. Not only that, but the game provides with an outline of the instructor that you can step in, so that you can follow his movements from his own perspective! 

I think this is the future of martial arts training, I feel it would be great to train for grading, and although it obviously does not substitute sparring at the gym it surely makes your technique more solid before you get there!

Thanks to the developer! Looking forward to Karate :)

I have been so bored with these so called Martial Arts training games or apps.... But THIS!!!! THIS IS EXACTLY WHAT I WANT!!! PLEASE PLEASE PLEASE THROW ALL MARTIAL ARTS ON THERE!!! Kung Fu Karate Muay Thai etc etc... This is the BEST Martial Arts training app I've ever seen! I'd give 10 stars if I could! AWESOME JOB GUYS THANK YOU FOR THIS!!! 🙏🙏
A very realistic experience.

Finally a mixed reality application that actually makes good use of it! So many gimmicky MR apps out there. 
I’m pretty new to karate, so being able to see my body while I’m going through routines is absolutely crucial. This app delivers on it.

Highly recommend giving it a shot. Miles better than other regular VR karate trainers. MR makes all the difference.

reviews.review_title: Best use of passthrough I've seen yet.
reviews.review_description: Excellent way to teach the correct positions. Now can we have a Yoga app that works the same way. :)

reviews.review_title: Great idea in this AR exercise game. 👏
reviews.review_description:
First, thank you developers for this, I liked it and enjoyed very much.
I did my karate 30 years ago,  this app help me to enjoy it again at 64 year old.  Thank you

reviews.review_title: A must for the martial artist!
reviews.review_description: This is the kind of martial art VR/MR app I have wanted for years. At the moment it is for Taekwondo however the developer is looking at adding karate also. This is so much better than practising kata by watching YouTube videos and trying to figure out what you should be doing by looking at different video angles. This is like being in a class being shown how to do techniques and you can learn kata quickly with this method that is very well implemented. Download this app and demo it at your dojo and support this devs great work

reviews.review_title: Try it. He's standing right there
reviews.review_description: Did the first lesson and legs are sore. Very effective and can learn a lot with this. I'm not into martial arts but this was good.

Originally started using this to get a refresher on my Chang-Hon forms, and discovered soo much more. Only reason it's not 5 star is due to the limited number of forms in the Chang-Hon pattern system. I imagine that will be updated eventually. Regardless can't recommend this enough for any martial artist trying to get in some additional practice at home.
